data.title: Gold Rallies to One-Month High Following Fed Rate Cut
data.description:
Gold futures are trading higher on Thursday and have reclaimed the $4,300 level after the Fed voted to cut interest rates by 25 bps. The precious metal is up by 4.7% over the past month and is currently trading at a one-month high.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Lower interest rates typically benefit gold because it doesn't pay out interest. Another way to put it is the opportunity cost of holding gold relative to interest-bearing assets, like Treasuries, goes down with lower rates.

Gold on Track for Best Year Since 1979

The Fed has lowered rates by a total of 75 bps this year in three separate Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings and is scheduled to meet again on January 28.

"Inflation hasn't really come back down to the Fed's 2% target, so, when you're lowering rates in an inflationary environment that is still not optimum, and that's very bullish for gold," said Marex analyst Edward Meir.

Spot gold (XAUUSD) has returned 60% year-to-date and is on track to cement its best year since 1979. The World Gold Council expects upside of 15% to 30% in 2026, driven by geopolitical risks, lower rates, and demand from exchange-traded funds (ETFs).

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Trump Faces Record Low Approval on Handling the Economy, Survey Shows
data.description:
31% of adults approve of President Trump's handling of the economy, marking a low for both his first and second term, according to a survey conducted by the Associated Press and the National Opinion Research Center (NORC). The survey includes responses from 1,146 adults between December 4 and 8.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Trump's economic management approval rating has steadily declined since reaching a second-term peak of 43% in August. Democrats and Republicans are sharply divided, with just 7% of Democrats approving compared with 69% of Republicans.

Unemployment and Sticky Inflation Highlight Economic Troubles

There are several indicators to gauge the health of the economy, such as the unemployment rate and inflation. As of September, the unemployment rate stood at 4.4%, a year-to-date high. In addition, inflation continues to be an issue for Americans and has stubbornly remained above the Fed's target of 2%.

The rating comes as several economists describe the economy as "K-shaped," which occurs when different areas of the economy experience different outcomes. The Fed's recent Beige Book report emphasized higher-income Americans driving spending, while low- and middle-income Americans are cutting back.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Rivian Stock (RIVN) Sinks after Revealing Its New Chip and Robotaxi Plans
data.description:
During its first "Autonomy and AI Day" in Palo Alto, EV maker Rivian Automotive (RIVN) announced that it built a custom chip, a new vehicle computer, and artificial intelligence models to bring self-driving features to its future cars. Starting in early 2026, Rivian plans to launch a subscription service called Autonomy+, which will offer autonomous driving capabilities powered by its new in-house processors and computer systems.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

The Autonomy+ service will be sold as a one-time $2,500 option or a monthly subscription starting at $49.99. For comparison, Tesla's (TSLA) premium Full Self-Driving (FSD) system costs $8,000 upfront or $99/month. In addition, Rivian says that an upcoming software update will include hands-free driving on over 3.5 million miles of marked roads across North America. Importantly, unlike Tesla, Rivian plans to use lidar and radar sensors to support Level 4 automation.

This would allow a car to drive itself under normal conditions while the passenger sleeps, similar to Waymo's robotaxis. Moreover, CEO RJ Scaringe said that the company will start with personally owned vehicles but aims to expand into robotaxis and ride-sharing in the future. This shift comes as EV sales slow down in the U.S. and global competition rises. And while the stock is up 25% this year, it's still down over 80% from its 2021 IPO and down over 7% at the time of writing.

Is RIVN Stock a Buy or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on RIVN stock based on five Buys, eight Holds, and six Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average RIVN price target of $13.87 per share implies 15.5% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: ChowChow Cloud Stock Craters 70% from Sept IPO in Mysterious Sell-Off
data.description:
Shares in Hong Kong-based cloud solutions provider ChowChow Cloud (CHOW) have tumbled by over 70% from their Initial Public Offering (IPO) price of $4.00 per share in mid-September.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

As of 1:17 p.m. EST on Thursday, CHOW dropped by 33.88%, adding to a loss of more than 84% from the previous day's trading session. In response to the development, ChowChow Cloud clarified that there has been "no [new] material development" in its business operations to cause such a plunge.

CHOW's Plunge Coincides with Oracle Q2 Selloff

The drop coincides with falling stock prices seen among specialized AI cloud capacity providers such as CoreWeave (CRWV), Nebius (NBIS), and IREN (IREN), and AI chipmakers such as Nvidia (NVDA), Broadcom (AVGO), and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), following cloud and software giant Oracle's (ORCL) disappointing Q2 2026 earnings results.

"The company has made inquiries and has been unable to determine whether corrective actions are appropriate at this time," ChowChow noted in a statement.

What Does ChowChow Do?

ChowChow provides solutions that enable businesses to run on the cloud by designing, migrating, and managing clients' technology infrastructure. The company focuses on the Asia-Pacific region, especially Hong Kong and Singapore.

As an information technology company, ChowChow is not a hardware-focused AI infrastructure provider like CoreWeave and Nebius. However, it deploys AI to provide proactive and automated maintenance of its clients' cloud environments. It also uses AI to monitor and optimize its cloud management services.

ChowChow started trading on the NYSE American on September 17, raising $10.4 million in gross proceeds from its IPO. NYSE American, unlike the main NYSE exchange, is where businesses with small market capitalization are listed.

Is CHOW Stock a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

On Wall Street, ChowChow's shares do not have analyst coverage.

However, TipRanks' Technical Analysis page indicates that the stock's Overall Consensus/Technical Sentiment over the past month is Strong Sell. This is based on four Bearish and one Neutral sentiment.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: S&P 500 Could Stumble to 6,500 in 2026, Warns Stifel
data.description:
Financial services company Stifel believes there is a 25% chance that the U.S. economy will enter a recession next year, which would bring the S&P 500 (SPX) to 6,500, implying downside of roughly 5% from current levels.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Stifel Chief Equity Strategist Barry Banister expects the benchmark index to trade in a range between 6,500 and 7,500 next year. The upper end of the range implies upside of 9%, which is "just the average annual S&P 500 price gain in the past 60 years."

Stifel Highlights Growth Potential but Flags Valuation and Labor Risks

Stifel's bull case points to EPS growth in the low teens and "one multiple of normal S&P 500 P/E compression."

However, the firm notes several risks, such as a weak labor market and elevated valuations. In addition, gross domestic product (GDP) could take a hit if Americans cut back on spending. To reduce risk, Stifel recommends hedging tech exposure with companies in the defensive growth and value sectors, such as healthcare, consumer staples, and gold.

data.title: "Tessie Always Got My Back": Tesla Stock (NASDAQ:TSLA) Slips as Registered Nurse Reveals Falling Asleep While Tesla Drives
data.description:
Electric vehicle giant Tesla (TSLA) has been pushing its Full Self-Driving (FSD) system like nobody's business, and it turns out that customers are all in favor of it as well. But some may be taking this notion to dangerous extremes, as revealed by one registered nurse who put a lot of faith in FSD. The news gave Tesla investors pause, and sent shares down modestly in Thursday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

We know that FSD needs to be supervised. But the thing we know in our heads and the experiences we live often say two different things. That dichotomy is where registered nurse Vanessa Lim lives, based on her recent post to TikTok. Lim drove home after a long night shift that left her exhausted, and she essentially turned the driving over to her Tesla, filming herself seemingly asleep behind the wheel.

The reaction was swift and varied. Some called the video a fake, noting Lim was "blinking" during the video. Others took issue with the recklessness of it, insisting that Lim was "…play(ing) with peoples lives." And while failsafes are supposedly in place to prevent such behavior, the failsafes have been circumvented in the past, reports note. Sometimes the failsafes even generate false positives, alerting about distraction even while being "fully focused on their surroundings."

Watch Those Batteries

Meanwhile, the Powerwall battery system is also coming under attack, as a new class-action lawsuit goes after the Powerwall 2 system. Arthur Brown filed the lawsuit in Florida federal court, which alleges that Powerwall 2 systems are sold with "…a defect that can cause them to overheat, emit smoke or catch fire."

The suit alleges that Tesla knew about this defect, but sold the systems anyway. The fact that Tesla staged a recall of the Powerwall 2 back in 2023 was brushed aside as "inadequate." Brown concludes the suit by noting that Tesla is "…guilty of breach of warranty, unjust enrichment and violations of Florida's consumer protection laws."

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 12 Holds, and 10 Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 7.98% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $383.54 per share implies 14.07% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: CoreWeave (CRWV) Partners with Runway to Power Advanced AI Video Models
data.description:
AI cloud provider CoreWeave (CRWV) has partnered with AI company Runway to support its advanced video generation tools. As part of the agreement, Runway will use CoreWeave's access to chipmaker Nvidia's (NVDA) powerful GB300 NVL72 systems to train and run its large AI models. Runway will also rely on CoreWeave's W&B Models to closely monitor how their systems perform during both training and deployment.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Unsurprisingly, CoreWeave says that more businesses are building tools and products around AI, so they need cloud platforms that deliver high speed, security, and scalability. Furthermore, Co-founder Brian Venturo praised Runway as a leader in generative video, not just in the tech world but also in top Hollywood studios. He added that CoreWeave is proud to support Runway as it continues to expand across more industries.

At the same time, Runway's CTO, Anastasis Germanidis, explained that the firm's technology is transforming industries from filmmaking to simulation training. However, in order to keep innovating, Runway needs cloud tools that are fast, reliable, and able to grow with its needs. As a result, he said that CoreWeave's platform will help the firm move quickly from research to real-world use, thereby making this partnership a key part of its long-term strategy.

Is CRWV Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on CRWV stock based on 13 Buys, 10 Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average CRWV price target of $133.77 per share implies 56% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Rallies as U.S. Trade Deficit Improvement Offsets Tech Weakness
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is climbing higher on Thursday, while both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are in the red on weakness in the tech sector.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

The Bureau of Economic Analysis announced that the U.S. trade deficit fell to a five-year low of $52.8 billion in September as exports increased by 3% to $289.4 billion, while imports rose by 0.6% to $342.1 billion. The deficit is calculated by subtracting exports from imports. The falling deficit was driven by increased exports of non-monetary gold and consumer goods and lower imports of computers, computer accessories, and electronics. Notably, the deficit with China plunged by $4 billion to $11.4 billion, with imports falling by $3.9 billion. In addition, the U.S. ran a trade surplus with Switzerland, the United Kingdom, Australia, and Brazil.

Meanwhile, initial jobless claims for the week ended December 11 grew by 44,000 to 236,000, registering the highest weekly growth since 2020. At the same time, economists note that the growth should be taken with a grain of salt because the previous week included the Thanksgiving holiday, which usually skews the data. Initial jobless claims for the week ended November 29 were at a three-year low.

"Don't read too much into the jump in jobless claims," said Navy Federal Credit Union Chief Economist Heather Long. "Smoothing it out, this still looks like an economy averaging 215,000 to 220,000 new jobless claims a week. That's not a cause for concern."

Finally, U.S. tariff revenue fell for the first time on a monthly basis since President Trump's first tariff order in February, with November's customs duties collection slipping to $30.76 billion from $31.35 billion. Amid an affordability crisis, Trump has stepped back from his aggressive tariff stance and excluded several grocery items from reciprocal tariffs last month. He also said that he would consider additional tariff carveouts.

The Dow Jones is up by 1.27% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Starbucks Stock (NASDAQ:SBUX) Notches Up Despite Hiring Discrimination Lawsuit
data.description:
Coffee giant Starbucks (SBUX) and its labor problems are absolutely not a secret at this point. Starbucks has been sued and struck in so many different directions that it is frankly a wonder anyone can get a cup of coffee anymore. And one more lawsuit just hit, as Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier takes aim at Starbucks over its DEI hiring practices. The news did not faze shareholders, though, who sent Starbucks shares up modestly in Thursday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Uthmeier's lawsuit claims that Starbucks engaged in "race-based quotas" when it came to hiring employees for what Starbucks calls the best job in retail. While the lawsuit does not point to any one incident, or individual, it does note that Starbucks spent the last five years "…in a pattern or practice of discrimination."

The lawsuit claims that Starbucks set up racial quotas in its hiring, paid employees differently based on their race, and connected mentorship programs "…only to a person of certain favored races" while denying access to those same programs for "…people of disfavored races." Uthmeier noted, "Starbucks made DEI more than a slogan. They turned it into a mandatory hiring and promotion system based upon race. The coffee empire set numerical racial targets for their workforce and they tied executive bonuses to those targets."

Scheduling Focus

Meanwhile, CEO Brian Niccol is taking aim at other processes at Starbucks, trying to smooth out the ordering system to try and take some of the weight off baristas. Niccol seems well-aware that the pick-up only locations served a valuable purpose: to take weight off the order-in cafe locations.

To that end, Niccol is looking to scheduling tools—he calls them Smart Queue systems—to try and sort orders. This helps the pickup orders go out through a drive-thru in a timely fashion, but helps ensure that the customers in the cafe aren't waiting overly long for their orders. The idea is a noble one, but trying to simply sort orders does not seem like it will solve the problem that the order-only locations were moving to fix: keeping orders from stacking up while baristas try to keep up.

Is Starbucks Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on SBUX stock based on 12 Buys, seven Holds and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 14.28% loss in its share price over the past year, the average SBUX price target of $95 per share implies 11.87% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson
